mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) reflect increasing consumer demand, which boosts corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. This is a direct demand-driven signal that does not depend on thresholds or interactions.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change with forward 20-day returns is not significantly positive over any 3-year rolling window (p > 0.05), or if the strategy loses money in backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market tightening and economic strength, leading to positive equity returns in the short term as investors respond to improving macro conditions.
failure_condition: If forward 5-day returns following the trigger are not positive on average (t-test p > 0.1) or if the trigger frequency is too low to be tradable (fewer than 10 occurrences in the evaluation window).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated and the Fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains very low, real interest rates turn negative, encouraging equity investment over bonds and boosting S&P 500 returns over the next month. This interaction captures the monetary stance effect on risk appetite.
regime_hint: low rate regime with moderate inflation
failure_condition: If negative real rate periods (CPI > 2%, FedFunds < 1%) do not produce positive forward 20-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the average return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) regime where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, highly accommodative monetary policy reduces discount rates for equities and supports valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon. This regime-conditional signal is based on the persistent low-rate environment.
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return during ZIRP regimes is not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.05) or if it is lower than in non-ZIRP periods during the same sample.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals rising manufacturing output and economic vigor, which translates into positive corporate earnings and higher S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month. This threshold-based signal activates only when growth exceeds a robust pace.
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return following a trigger is not higher than the unconditional average (one-sided t-test p > 0.1) or if the trigger misses more than 50% of strong positive return periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in housing starts (HOUST) and unemployment (UNRATE) — with starts rising and joblessness falling — indicates a robust housing market and economic recovery, providing a powerful joint signal for positive S&P 500 returns over a longer one-quarter horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month increase > 5% and UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0.1 percentage points
failure_condition: If the forward 60-day return following simultaneous positive HOUST and UNRATE signals is not positive in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the average return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Steady real GDP growth (GDPC1) anchors long-term corporate earnings expectations and drives equity valuations higher. This always-on trend signal uses the quarterly GDP release to capture the fundamental pace of economic expansion, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between GDPC1 quarterly growth and forward 60-day returns is below 0.10 over any 5-year period, or if the strategy underperforms a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust job creation and consumer income growth, boosting market sentiment and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week. This threshold-triggered signal captures the market's immediate reaction to employment strength.
failure_condition: If the average forward 5-day return after a trigger is not positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.1) or if the positive predictive accuracy is below 55% over any 2-year window.
